outlook.headline: Agentic and inference demand is broadening fast into 2026Q1
outlook.summary: The read-through is clearly accelerating: mention momentum rose to 3.8x latest from a 1.7x starting point, and every company cited agentic/inference AI as a positive this quarter. Demand is no longer confined to GPUs and custom silicon; it is spreading into servers, memory, networking, EDA, test, storage, and edge devices, with several suppliers also noting tight or secured supply that supports near-term upside.

drivers.label: Inference/agentic workload inflection
drivers.detail: Multiple companies said inference and agentic AI are inflecting faster and becoming a larger share of AI activity, lifting demand across compute platforms and adjacent silicon.

drivers.label: Broader content per system
drivers.detail: The shift is increasing silicon content in memory, timing, power, probes, and CPU importance, which expands demand intensity beyond the accelerator itself.

drivers.label: Data center and manufacturing pull-through
drivers.detail: Agentic AI is translating into real build activity in wafers, equipment, and supporting infrastructure, signaling that demand is already moving from interest to orders and ramps.

drivers.label: Use-case expansion beyond cloud
drivers.detail: Inference is reaching consumers, enterprises, robotics, voice, and edge devices, widening the demand base and reducing reliance on a single hyperscale cycle.

drivers.label: Supply tightness validating upside
drivers.detail: Where companies called out tight supply or secured supply, it reinforces that demand is running ahead of available capacity in the near term.

risks: A faster-than-expected normalization in supply could cap the pace of near-term upside despite strong demand.
If enterprise and consumer agentic deployments take longer to scale, the demand broadening could stay concentrated in early adopters.
Any pause in data-center build plans would quickly show up in wafers, memory, networking, and test orders.
